operating_state: Total revenue reached $201.0 billion in 2025, representing 22% growth driven by advertising revenue across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and WhatsApp. Ad impressions increased 12% globally while average price per ad rose 9%, with Family daily active people growing 7% to 3.58 billion. Reality Labs revenue increased 3% driven by AI glasses sales partially offset by lower Meta Quest sales. European regulatory developments including DMA final decision against subscription model have forced product modifications that degrade user experience and reduce monetization capabilities.
cost_structure: Total costs increased 24% driven by research and development expenses rising 31% from 8% headcount growth in engineering functions and AI infrastructure investments. Cost of revenue increased 20% primarily from higher data center and technical infrastructure expenses, partially offset by extended server useful lives to 5.5 years reducing depreciation. Reality Labs operating losses reduced overall operating profit, with similar losses expected in 2026.
material_events: European Commission issued final decision in April 2025 ruling subscription model violates DMA requirements, prompting appeal and implementation of less personalized advertising options. Completed share repurchases and increased quarterly dividend to $0.525 per share. Issued senior unsecured notes and entered joint venture contributing Louisiana data center assets. One Big Beautiful Bill Act enacted July 2025 triggered tax charge including valuation allowance against deferred tax assets due to Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax limitations.
macro_exposures: Regulatory enforcement escalated with European Commission imposing fines for DMA violations and competition law breaches. Apple iOS changes and browser restrictions continue negatively impacting ad targeting capabilities, with similar restrictions expected from other platforms. Russian government restrictions following Ukraine war and Middle East conflicts contributed to user decreases and advertising revenue impacts. EU-US Data Privacy Framework invalidation could prevent offering Facebook and Instagram in Europe.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to support AI initiatives and core infrastructure, with Reality Labs losses remaining similar to 2025 levels. Approximately 70% of Reality Labs expenses will focus on wearables initiatives with remaining 30% on VR development. Key investment areas include AI, Reels monetization, youth engagement, and infrastructure capacity expansion.
uncertainty_profile: Youth-related litigation seeks damages potentially reaching high tens of billions with first trials beginning January 2026, alongside over 100,000 individual arbitration demands. FTC administrative proceedings seek substantial consent order modifications including minor data use restrictions and product launch limitations. Multiple copyright infringement cases related to AI training proceed to mid-2027 trials with potential statutory damages. IRS transfer pricing disputes continue with additional assessments, while European DSA compliance proceedings remain ongoing.
